Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-06 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum ranker shows CLMT and CENX as the top two performers, but with distinctly different risk profiles. CLMT (rank 1, score 95.6) demonstrates remarkably consistent momentum across all timeframes—up 171.63% over one year with a perfect 4/4 ratio passes and exceptional resilience on bad SPY days (0.9864 score)—making it the cleaner technical setup. CENX, by contrast, shows explosive absolute returns (246.99% in one year, 116.43% in six months) and stronger outperformance versus SPY/QQQ, but carries a concerning red flag: its bad_spy_score of -1.0018 indicates it tends to decline sharply during market downturns, suggesting its gains are concentrated in favorable conditions rather than built on stability. Both clear all SMA gates (trading above their 30/50/100/200 day averages), but CLMT's superior momentum_signal (-0.62 vs likely negative for CENX) and balanced correlation profile make it structurally more resilient, while CENX's performance appears more leveraged to sector strength and market risk appetite.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
